items.text:
DINING HALL ALLERGEN POLICY UPDATE

Effective Monday, September 8, the Anderson Dining Hall will adopt a revised allergen policy designed to better protect students with food sensitivities. All hot-station menus will be labeled with the top nine allergens, and a dedicated allergen-free station will be opened at lunch.

Key changes include:

- Allergen ingredient cards will be displayed at each station from 7:00 AM to 9:00 PM
- A separate allergen-free prep counter will operate at the south entrance
- Students with documented allergies can request meals prepared by the head chef with 24 hours notice
- The new allergen-free meal plan add-on costs $45 per semester and can be activated through the dining portal

Students are encouraged to register their dietary needs with Ms. Garner at the Dining Services Office, Room 110, before September 5. Late registration is accepted but cannot be guaranteed for the first week.

Questions should be directed to dining@university.edu.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the cost of the new allergen-free meal plan add-on?

options.A: A charge of twenty dollars per semester
options.B: A charge of thirty dollars per semester
options.C: A charge of sixty dollars per semester
options.D: A charge of forty-five dollars per semester

questions.explanation: The notice states the allergen-free meal plan add-on costs $45 per semester.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where should students with documented allergies register their dietary needs?

options.A: At the south entrance allergen-free prep counter
options.B: At the dining services portal with the head chef
options.C: At the Dining Services Office in Room 110
options.D: At the Anderson Dining Hall information desk

questions.explanation: The notice instructs students to register with Ms. Garner at the Dining Services Office, Room 110.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who register after September 5?

options.A: They will be charged a higher fee for the meal plan
options.B: They will be required to attend an in-person interview
options.C: They may not receive accommodations the first week
options.D: They must contact the head chef directly each meal

questions.explanation: The notice says late registration is accepted but cannot be guaranteed for the first week.

items.text:
LAUNDRY ROOM RENOVATION NOTICE

The basement laundry rooms in Hartley Residence Hall will be temporarily closed for renovation from Monday, October 14 through Friday, November 1. During this period, all washers and dryers will be replaced with newer high-efficiency units, and electrical wiring will be upgraded.

Students should be aware of the following arrangements:

- The Bryant Hall laundry room, located two blocks east, will remain open 24 hours for Hartley residents during the closure
- A free shuttle will run between Hartley and Bryant every twenty minutes from 6:00 PM to 11:00 PM on weekdays
- Any laundry left in the Hartley machines on October 13 will be relocated to the lost-and-found bin in the lobby for one week before being discarded
- Residents needing emergency laundry service can contact Mr. Sutton at the Housing Office

The new machines will offer mobile-pay access through the housing app, eliminating the need for coin purchases. The renovation is being completed by Carter Facilities, the contractor selected by Residential Services.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen to laundry left in Hartley machines on October 13?

options.A: It will be moved to the lobby lost-and-found
options.B: It will be transferred to Bryant Hall machines
options.C: It will be held for collection by Mr. Sutton
options.D: It will be discarded immediately by Carter Facilities

questions.explanation: The notice says laundry left on October 13 will be relocated to the lost-and-found bin in the lobby.

questions.stem: The word "eliminating" is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: The mobile-pay feature does away with the need for coin purchases entirely, so "eliminating" means removing.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Hartley residents during the renovation period?

options.A: They will be reimbursed for laundry costs at Bryant Hall
options.B: They will need to walk or use the shuttle to do laundry
options.C: They can request in-room pickup from the Housing Office
options.D: They will receive priority access at all campus laundry rooms

questions.explanation: Since the Hartley laundry rooms are closed, residents must travel to Bryant Hall — either walking the two blocks or using the shuttle.
